svg.text: 41-Scene Generation Templates
I. Function Overview

Scene Generation Templates let creators quickly generate large-scale scenes. These templates allow customization of what gets generated and their quantities, facilitating rapid construction of diverse scenes.
II. Using Preset Templates
1. Entry Point
Go to Entity Placement, open Scene Generation Templates, then Preset Templates. Pick a template and confirm to import.
2. Generate Entities
In the template tab, click or drag a template to place an entity in the scene.
3. Adjust Entity Parameters
Select the entity. In Base Attributes, configure generation rules and generation range. Open Asset Configuration to fine-tune asset types, density, and other parameters.
4. Generate and Adjust
Click Random Preview to preview the result. Click Confirm Generation to create objects. After generation, use Select All or Delete to batch manage generated objects.
III. Creating Scene Generation Templates
1. Function Entry
Go to Entity Placement, open Scene Generation Templates, then click Manage Template. Or open the system menu and click Scene Generation Template Management.
2. Create and Configure
Click New Scene Generation Template. Click Asset Configuration to set parameters. After saving, the template is available in the Asset panel under Scene Generation Templates.
IV. Parameters Introduction
Base Attributes Panel
Contains Generation Rules and Generation Range.
Generation Rules
Scatter Rule Preset List: click Random Preview to preview scattering. Save the rule and reuse it from the preset list.
Lock Generation Result: when enabled, every generation repeats the previous result exactly.
Generation Range
Spawn Area Shape: sets the shape of the spawn area. Two options: Cuboid and Cylinder.
Asset Configuration Panel
Edit parameters for each spawn layer. Five default layers: Rock, Building, Tree, Vegetation, Decoration. Custom layers can also be added.
Layer Parameters
Generation Density: controls crowding. Lower values = sparser, higher values = denser.
Generation Randomness: controls sampling-point jitter. 0 = more uniform, 1 = more chaotic.
Generate Based on Terrain: Rock Layer only. Controls whether rocks generate based on scene terrain.
Associated Range: Vegetation Layer only. Defines generation radius around Tree Layer objects.
Detailed Layer Editing
To fine-tune a layer (asset types, weights, spawn interval, transform ranges), click Details Editing.
Weight: controls how often each model is chosen within the same layer.
Generate Interval Distance: controls offset distance between current model and objects generated in the next layer.
Model Assets: click Add Assets to add more assets from the Model Asset Library.
Transform Range: sets the jitter range for location, rotation, and scale.
V. Special Notes
Generation Order
Layers generate in order: Rock first, then Building, Tree, Vegetation, and so on.
Exclusion Rule
Except for Rock, each layer generates only in empty space after excluding the previous layer's objects. If a layer is too dense, later layers may have nowhere to spawn.
Vegetation Association
The Vegetation Layer follows the Associated Rule and spawns with the Tree Layer. If the Tree layer does not generate, the Vegetation layer will not generate either.
Generated by AI based on official Miliastra Academy documentation. Version: Luminary
